Scenario.title: Spearphishing-Driven Denial of Service Attack on Communications Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A sophisticated attack targeting communications infrastructure begins with spearphishing to gain initial access, followed by PowerShell-based execution and concludes with network-level denial of service to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Hacking Tool, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, PowerShell Script, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker crafts highly targeted phishing emails mimicking legitimate communication providers, embedding malicious hyperlinks that redirect victims to compromised websites. These links exploit trust relationships to deliver payloads to internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: After compromising credentials via spearphishing, attackers leverage PowerShell scripts to execute arbitrary commands on affected systems. This method avoids traditional malware detection by utilizing native scripting capabilities.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The final stage involves flooding network traffic with malformed packets or exploiting protocol weaknesses to overwhelm routers and switches. This causes complete disruption of communication services by rendering critical infrastructure inoperable.

Scenario.summary: A targeted attack leveraging spearphishing, PowerShell execution, and network flooding disrupts communications infrastructure through a straightforward yet effective multi-stage approach.
